notes: Aimpoint CompM5 red dot. 30 mm tube body. 2 MOA dot. Issued by US Army as the M150. Battery life 50,000 hours on one AAA. Eye relief is unlimited (typical of parallax-free red dots) so the column is null. Specs from manufacturer product page.
Arken EP5 5-25x56 FFP. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: VPR MIL, VPR MOA. Direct-to-consumer budget PRS scope. Specs from manufacturer product page.
Arken SH4 Gen 2 6-24x50 FFP. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: VPR MIL, VPR MOA. Specs from manufacturer product page.
Athlon Cronus BTR Gen2 4.5-29x56. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: APRS6 FFP IR MIL, APLR8 FFP IR MOA. Specs from the manufacturer product page.
Programmable Elevation Knob heads-up display scope.
Burris XTR III 5.5-30x56. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: SCR2 MIL, SCR2 MOA, Tremor3 MIL. Specs from the manufacturer product page.
Bushnell Match Pro ED 5-30x56 FFP. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: EQL (MIL Christmas tree), Deploy MIL, Deploy MOA. Specs from the manufacturer product page.

Zeiss's premium tactical FFP long-range scope. 34 mm main tube (62 mm objective tube, 46 mm eyepiece tube), 0.1 MRAD or 0.25 MOA clicks, 12 MIL / 30 MOA per rotation. Class-leading 40.7 MIL / 140 MOA elevation travel and 24 MIL / 60 MOA windage travel via Ballistic Stop turret. 90% light transmission. Eye relief 90 mm (3.54 in), length 396 mm (15.6 in), weight 1030 g (36.3 oz, body without inner rail). Parallax 25 m (27 yd) to infinity. Reticle options: ZF-MRi (FFP MRAD), ZF-MOAi (FFP MOA). Specs sourced verbatim from the official Zeiss product page.

Zeiss Victory V8 hunting flagship. 36 mm main tube (oversized for greater elevation range), 60 mm objective (effective lens diameter 48.1 mm). SFP. 92% light transmission with LotuTec + T* coatings. Click value published as 1 cm at 100 m (~0.36 MOA equivalent); max elevation 140 cm at 100 m (= 50.4 MOA), max windage 85 cm at 100 m (= 30.6 MOA). Eye relief 92 mm (3.6 in), length 399 mm (15.7 in), weight 995 g (35.1 oz, body without inner rail; 1015 g / 35.8 oz with inner rail). Parallax 50 m (55 yd) to infinity. Reticle options on the international SKUs: Reticle 43 (illuminated mil-dot variant), Reticle 60 (illuminated plex). The North American SKUs listed on sportsoptics-shop.zeiss.com also offer reticle 90 (AHR). The ZBR-2 / ZMOA-T30 reticles named in the LoadOut catalog sourcing brief are NOT cataloged for the V8 family on either zeiss.com region; use Reticle 60 as the default. Specs sourced verbatim from the international Zeiss product page.

model_name: March-FX 4.5-28x52 FFP Tactical High Master Wide-Angle
notes: March-FX 4.5-28x52 FFP Tactical (model code D28HV52WFIMLX), with the 25-degree wide-angle eyepiece, super ED 'High Master' lenses, Shuriken lock turrets, and Temperature Anti-Drift Lens System. 6.2x zoom ratio, 34 mm tube (4 mm wall), 52 mm objective. 0.1 MIL clicks, 10 MIL per turret rotation, 30 MIL total elevation, 20 MIL total windage. Eye relief 72-92 mm (~3.23 in mid-range), length 318 mm (12.5 in), weight 880 g (31.04 oz). Parallax 10 yd to infinity. Reticle options: FML-TR1 (illuminated MIL), FML-3 (illuminated MIL), FML-PDKi (illuminated MIL). FML-LDK / FML-2 / MTR-1 named in the LoadOut sourcing brief are not in the current FFP catalog for this body. Specs sourced from the official DEON marchscopes.com product page.

March-X Tactical 5-50x56 SFP (model code D50V56TI). 10x zoom ratio, 34 mm tube, 56 mm objective, ED glass, fully machined from aluminum ingot, argon filled. Click value 1/8 MOA (with 0-Set device), 10 MOA per turret rotation, 60 MOA total elevation, 40 MOA total windage. Eye relief 92-101 mm (~3.78 in mid-range), length 400 mm (15.75 in), weight 855 g (30.16 oz). Parallax 10 yd to infinity. Reticle options: MTR-1, MTR-2, MTR-3, MTR-4, MTR-5, MTR-FT, MTR-RTM, FD-1 (and Di-Plex on the older non-illuminated SKU). Note: the prompt brief named a 'High Master Wide-Angle FFP' 5-50x56, but DEON's HM Wide-Angle FFP family currently tops out at the 5-42x56 (D42HV56WFIMLX-G2); the 5-50x56 ships as SFP only. Specs sourced from the official DEON marchscopes.com product page.
EOTech XPS3-0 Holographic Weapon Sight. 1 MOA aiming dot inside a 68 MOA ring. Tube-less holographic design (tubeDiameterMm = 0). NV-compatible. Spec from manufacturer product page.
Element Optics Helix 6-24x50 FFP. 30 mm tube. Reticle options: APR-2D MIL, APR-2D MOA, EHR-2 MOA. Popular budget PRS scope.

stock_reticle: MOA Pro Ext / Mil Pro Ext

notes: Hensoldt ZF 4-16x56 FF/TPM. 34 mm tube. Mil only. Reticle options: CCC, NSV, MR, MR-DOT. Specs from manufacturer product page; metric converted (1035 g -> 36.5 oz, 380 mm -> 14.96 in).
Hensoldt ZF 6-24x72 FF/TPM. 34 mm tube, 72 mm objective for ELR. Reticle options: CCC, NSV, MR, MR-DOT. Metric converted (1180 g -> 41.6 oz, 410 mm -> 16.14 in).
Holosun HE509T-RD red dot. Open-emitter pistol optic. 32 MOA ring + 2 MOA dot reticle (multi-reticle option). Solar + battery dual power. Spec from manufacturer product page. Tubeless open emitter.
Kahles K318i 3.5-18x50 FFP. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: SKMR4, MSR/Ki, AMR, MOAK. Metric converted (850 g -> 30 oz, 350 mm -> 13.78 in).

Kahles K525i 5-25x56 FFP. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: SKMR3, SKMR4, MSR, AMR, MOAK. Hallmark 'Twist Guard Wheels' parallax + ergonomic CCW windage on Right side. Metric converted (925 g -> 32.6 oz, 382 mm -> 15.04 in).

Mid-tier 4:1 zoom FFP precision scope. 34 mm tube. M5C3 (0.1 MIL) and M1C3 (1/4 MOA) turret variants. Reticle options: PR2-MIL, PR2-MOA, PR1-MIL, PR1-MOA, TMR, illuminated PR1-MIL/MOA. Spec values from the leupold.com product page.

Flagship Mark 5HD FFP scope. 35 mm tube (rare for a US-built scope). M5C3 = 0.1 MIL turret, 3 revolutions, ZeroLock; M1C3 = 1/4 MOA turret, 3 revolutions, ZeroLock. Reticle options: PR2-MIL, PR2-MOA, PR1-MIL, PR1-MOA, TMR, FFP H59, Tremor 3 (MIL only). Travel-per-rev, max elevation, max windage values copied from the leupold.com product page (MIL turret variant).

Hunter-favorite 5:1 zoom SFP scope. 30 mm tube. Reticle options: HTMR, FireDot Duplex, Wind-Plex, Impact-29 MOA, Boone & Crockett, plain Duplex, HTMR-MIL (Gen 2). Sold with Leupold's Custom Dial System (CDS) ZeroLock turret -- 0.1 MIL clicks per the leupold.com product page. Specs from the product page.

Meopta Optika6 mid-tier 6x-zoom FFP scope. 30 mm tube, 50 mm objective, ELOX hard anodized finish, 91% daylight light transmission. Sold in MIL (0.1 MRAD click) and MOA (1/4 MOA click) SKUs. 24.4 MIL / 84 MOA total elev and wind adjustment. Eye relief 3.94 in, length 14.2 in, weight 31.0 oz. Parallax 25 yd to infinity. Reticle options: MRAD-1 (FFP MRAD, illuminated), Mil-Dot 2 / 4C (illuminated). The 'sportoptics.com' mirror lists a '0.36 MIL' click which is a typo of the 1 cm/100m equivalent; the manufacturer-published value is 0.1 MRAD. Specs sourced from gunmart.net Sporting Shooter review (cross-referenced against bhphotovideo, sportoptics, and pyramydair listings).

Meopta's flagship Optika6 long-range FFP scope. 34 mm tube, 56 mm objective, ED glass, 91% daylight light transmission, MeoBright coatings. 1 cm/100m click (= 0.1 MRAD), 320 cm/100m elev/wind range (= 32 MIL = 110 MOA total adjustment), exposed windage and zero-stop elevation. Eye relief 3.94 in, length 15.44 in, weight 36.7 oz. Parallax 25 yd to infinity. Reticles on the FFP RD SKU: MRAD-1 (illuminated FFP MRAD), Mil-Dot 3 (illuminated). Specs sourced from kenziesoptics.com retailer mirror (the official Meopta product page returned 404 at time of capture); cross-checked against airgundepot.com and everydaymarksman.co review for elevation/click.
Compact DMR-class FFP scope. Reticle options: MOAR F1, MIL-R F1, MIL-C F1, MIL-XT, Horus TREMOR3, MOA-XT. 34mm tube. Specs from the Nightforce product page.

Flagship long-range FFP scope. Sold in 7 reticle SKUs: MOAR F1, MIL-R F1, MIL-C F1, MIL-XT, Horus TREMOR3, MOA-XT, Horus TREMOR5. Click value, internal adjustment range, eye relief, length, weight and parallax taken verbatim from the Nightforce product spec sheet.
FFP LPVO, smaller package than ATACR.
Compact 8x-zoom F1 with 30mm tube. Reticle options: MOAR F1, MIL-C F1, MIL-XT, Horus TREMOR3, MOA-XT, Horus TREMOR5. Specs from the Nightforce product page.

Hunting / SFP scope with 30mm tube. Sold in MOAR (20 MOA scale below center) and Forceplex variants, illuminated and non-illuminated. Click value MOA only (no MRAD turret variant for this body). Specs from the Nightforce product page.
Primary Arms GLx 4-16x50 FFP. 30 mm tube. Reticle options: ACSS Griffin MIL, ACSS-HUD-DMR-308. Specs from manufacturer product page.
Primary Arms SLx 4-14x44 FFP. 30 mm tube. Reticle options: ACSS-HUD-DMR-308 / 6.5CM, MIL-DOT, ACSS Apollo 6.5GR/.224V. Specs from manufacturer product page.

Substituted for the prompt's nonexistent 'Riton 5 PRIMAL 5-25x56' (the Primal line is a hunting series and tops out below 25x; the 5-Series flagship at this size is the 5 Conquer). 34 mm tube, FFP, 0.1 MRAD or 1/4 MOA clicks (sold as separate MIL and MOA SKUs), 26 MIL or 90 MOA total adjustment, R5 zero-stop turrets, IPX7, 6061-T6 aluminum, 1200 G impact tested, mounting length 7.37 in. UPC: MOA 850041390093, MRAD 850041390086. Reticles: PSR (FFP MRAD, illuminated), BAS (FFP MOA, illuminated). Specs sourced from the Riton product page.

Riton 7-Series flagship long-range FFP scope. 34 mm tube, 8x zoom ratio (4-32x), 0.1 MRAD or 1/4 MOA clicks (separate SKUs), 32 MIL or 110 MOA total adjustment, R7 stainless-steel zero-stop turrets, parallax 20 yd to infinity. UPC: MRAD 850041390185, MOA 850041390192. Reticles: PSR (FFP MRAD, illuminated), MOR (FFP MOA, illuminated). Specs sourced from the Riton product page.

Standard-length tube variant of the S&B PM II 3-20x50 (paired with the Ultra Short variant — id `schmidt_bender_pm_ii_3_20x50_ultra_short` — which has a shorter tube for action-pistol use). Optical platform identical; mount compatibility differs.

Shorter-tube variant of the S&B PM II 3-20x50 (paired with the standard-length variant — id `schmidt_bender_pm_ii_3_20x50`). Designed for action-pistol use. Optical platform identical; mount compatibility differs.

Flagship Police Marksman II LP. 34 mm tube, 56 mm objective, 90 mm eye relief, 0.1 MRAD or 1/4 MOA clicks. Total elevation travel 27 MRAD on the DT27 turret variant (equivalent to ~92.7 MOA); windage +/-6 MRAD per side (12 MRAD total). Reticle options: P4F-MIL, P4F-MOA, P4FL, MSR2, GR2ID, H59, H37, H58, Horus TReMoR3. Weight, length, eye relief, and parallax-min taken from the official Schmidt & Bender product page; values converted from metric (1065 g, 420 mm, 90 mm, 10 m parallax-min) to imperial. Reticle catalog cross-checked against the schmidtundbender.de PM II 5-25 family page.
BDX system pairs with Sig rangefinder for solutions.
Sig Sauer TANGO6T 1-6x24 LPVO (US Army DVO selected). Reticle options: MSR-BDC6, MOA-MILQUAD, DWLR6, Hellfire BDC. SFP. Specs from manufacturer product page.
Sig Sauer TANGO6T DEV-L 5-30x56 FFP (USSOCOM-selected). 34 mm tube. Reticle options: DEV-L MRAD, DEV-L MIL Christmas tree.

Sightron SIII Precision Long Range (PLR) 6-24x50 FFP. 30 mm tube, 50 mm objective. Sold in MIL SKU (0.1 MRAD clicks, 10 MIL/rev, 23.3 MIL elev/wind) and MOA SKU (0.25 MOA clicks, 20 MOA/rev, 80 MOA elev/wind). Eye relief 3.6-3.8 in, length 15.4 in, weight 28.2 oz, parallax 20 m (22 yd) to infinity. 11-position illumination, CR2032 battery. Reticle options: MH-H IR (FFP MIL hash, illuminated), MOA-H IR (FFP MOA hash, illuminated). The IRMOA-H reticle named in the LoadOut sourcing brief is not in the current FFP PLR catalog; the SFP PLR variants offer different reticles. Specs sourced verbatim from the official Sightron product page.

model_name: SVIII 5-40x56 FFP ED IR Zero Stop
notes: Sightron's flagship FFP long-range scope (SVIII 5-40x56 FFP ED). 40 mm body tube (oversized vs. the 30/34 mm norm), 56 mm objective, ED glass, MC-777 fully multi-coated, 8x zoom ratio. 0.1 MRAD clicks, 10 MIL per rotation, 40 MIL total elevation, 20 MIL total windage. Internal zero stop. Eye relief 3.7-3.8 in, length 16.8 in, weight 49.0 oz. Parallax 10 m (11 yd) to infinity, IPX7 waterproof, nitrogen-charged. Reticle options: LRM (FFP MIL hash, illuminated), MH-6 (FFP mil-hash with mid-haul reference, illuminated). NOTE: the LoadOut sourcing brief named a 'Sightron SVII PLR 4.5-30x56' which does not exist; the SVIII 5-40x56 FFP is the closest cataloged FFP flagship. The SIII PLR 6-24x50 FFP entry below covers the smaller PLR family member. Specs sourced verbatim from the official Sightron product page.

Swarovski's tactical / long-range SFP flagship. 30 mm tube, 56 mm objective, illuminated SFP reticle, 1/4 MOA tactical turrets (also offered with 1/8 MOA and 0.5 cm/100m click variants under different SKUs). 80 clicks per rotation (20 MOA per rev), 86.4 MOA total elevation (with 40-click sub-zero), 54 MOA windage. Patent-pending spring retention enables higher adjustment range than typical SFP scopes. Parallax 50 m (55 yd) to infinity, length 376 mm (14.8 in), weight 910 g (32.1 oz). Reticle options: 4WX-I+, BRX-I+, BRH-I+, Plex-I+, BRMm-I (illuminated). Specs sourced from greentop.com retailer mirror (Swarovski USA product page returned 404 at time of capture); cross-referenced with Gun Mart review and Optics-Trade listing.

Premium Austrian hunting scope (Z6i Gen 2). 30 mm tube, 56 mm objective, illuminated SFP reticle. 1.0 cm/100m click value (= ~0.36 MOA = 0.1 MRAD numerically), 64.8 MOA total elevation, 36 MOA total windage. Eye relief 3.74 in, length 14.45 in (367 mm), weight 23.1 oz (655 g) -- significantly lighter than the X5i flagship. Parallax 50 m (55 yd) to infinity. Reticle options on the BT (Ballistic Turret) variant: 4A-I (default), BRX-I, BRH-I, BRT-I (catalog includes BRT, BRX, 4A-IF in non-Gen2 SKUs but the current Gen-2 catalog confirms 4A-I as standard). Specs sourced from sportoptics.com retailer mirror; Swarovski direct URL returned 404 at time of capture.
Tangent Theta Mid-Range (TT315M) 3-15x50. 34 mm tube. Reticle options: JTAC, Gen3XR, GRSC, MIL, MOA. Metric values converted (920 g -> 32.45 oz, 343 mm -> 13.5 in).

Flagship Tangent Theta 5-25x56 'Professional' (TT525P). 34 mm tube, 56 mm objective. Reticle options: JTAC, Gen3XR, GRSC, MIL, MOA, Mil-Trig. Specs from the manufacturer product page; metric values converted (1045 g -> 36.85 oz, 396 mm -> 15.6 in, 90 mm eye relief -> 3.54 in). Total elevation 33 MRAD; total windage 16 MRAD.

Trijicon ACOG TA31 4x32 fixed-power prism. BAC reticle (Bindon Aiming Concept) with red illuminated chevron at 100 yd zero. Tritium + fiber optic illumination. Iconic infantry combat optic. Spec from manufacturer product page. Tubeless design (tubeDiameterMm = 0).
Lighter Gen II for hunting and PRS-light.

Workhorse FFP precision rifle scope. Sold in 6 reticle SKUs spanning EBR-7C MRAD/MOA, EBR-2C MRAD/MOA, and the Horus-licensed H59 MRAD and TReMoR3 MRAD variants. MRAD spec (RZR-42708) sourced from sportoptics.com product page; MOA spec (RZR-42707) cross-checked. MOA travel-per-rev value from the manufacturer's L-Tec turret system (25 MOA/rev consistent with 10 mil/rev).

Flagship long-range FFP scope. Sold in MRAD (RZR-63600) and MOA variants under one base model. Spec values cross-checked against vortexoptics.com product page and the Vortex Razor HD Gen III review aggregator (specs sourced 2026-05-09).

ZCO ZC527 5-27x56. 36 mm tube (rare; matches ZC840). Reticle options: MPCT2, MPCT3, MPCT3X (MIL); MOA (Tremor3 special order). Specs from manufacturer product page; metric converted (1130 g -> 39.8 oz, 405 mm -> 15.94 in).

ZCO ZC840 8-40x56 ELR. 36 mm tube. 0.05 MRAD or 1/8 MOA fine clicks. Reticle options: MPCT2, MPCT3, MPCT3X. Specs from manufacturer product page; metric converted (1165 g -> 41.1 oz, 417 mm -> 16.42 in).

Australian-designed FFP precision scope (Trace Advanced family). 30 mm tube, 50 mm objective, 0.1 MRAD clicks, 17.8 MIL elev/wind travel, IPX7 waterproof, FMC coatings, 92% light transmission. Eye relief 3.58-3.94 in (91-100 mm), parallax 25 m (27 yd) to infinity. Reticle ships with the RMG (FFP MRAD) pattern; the SFP-bodied 4.5-27x50 R3 is a separate model. Length not published on the product page. Specs sourced from the official ZeroTech product page (zerotechoptics.com/p/4-5-27x50mm-rmg-trace-riflescope/).

Mid-tier hunting / target SFP scope. 30 mm tube, 50 mm objective, 0.25 MOA Pop-Lok turrets, 70 MOA elev/wind travel, eye relief 3.5 in, parallax 20 m (22 yd) to infinity, weight 28.2 oz. Reticle options: PHR II, PHR II Illuminated, R3, R3 Illuminated. (Note: PHR III is a sibling reticle ZeroTech sells on the Thrive line, not the Vengeance.) Length not published on the product page. Specs sourced from the official ZeroTech product page.
Entry-level hunting SFP. Phase 5 Class A→B promotion: confirmed REAL_CURRENT_SKU via Vortex product page (May 2026).
Mid-tier hunting SFP. Phase 5 Class A→B promotion: confirmed REAL_CURRENT_SKU via Bushnell product page (May 2026); FOV 30 ft @ 3x / 6 ft @ 12x @ 100 yd.
LPVO Gen 2. Phase 5 Class A→B promotion: confirmed REAL_CURRENT_SKU via Vortex product page (May 2026); FOV 116.5 ft @ 1x / 19.2 ft @ 6x @ 100 yd.

Hunting SFP. Phase 5 Class A→B promotion: confirmed REAL_CURRENT_SKU via Trijicon product page (May 2026). Note: a 2.5-15x56 variant also exists in catalog; this is the 42 mm objective variant per Appendix G #32.

LPVO holographic-style SFP. Phase 5 Class A→B promotion: confirmed REAL_CURRENT_SKU via EOTech product page (May 2026). EOTech ALSO ships a 1-6x24 FFP variant (already in catalog at vortex_optics_…—wait, EOTech VUDU 1-6x24 FFP); the FFP and SFP are distinct SKUs.

Hensoldt FFP flagship tactical. **Substituted for brief Appendix G #8** "ZF 5-25x56" — that exact mag range does NOT exist as a Hensoldt SKU per Phase 5 verification (May 2026); the current flagship FFP is ZF 3.5-26x56 (36 mm tube, 0.1 MRAD clicks). Phase 6 errata updates Appendix G accordingly.
ZCO 5-27x56 FFP flagship. Phase 5 Class B confirmed addition (May 2026 verification); FOV 21 ft @ 5x.

Burris AR-332 fixed-3x prism sight for AR-15. Phase 5 Class B confirmed addition (May 2026 verification); CR2032 battery. Stored as `category: prism-sight` since prism housings don't carry a tube diameter.

Phase 5 Class C dual-reticle split: the ATACR 5-25x56 F1 ships in BOTH Mil-XT (existing scope row mapped to loadout_mil_tree_flare) AND MOAR-T (this row, mapped to loadout_moa_tree_flare). Adding the second row preserves the existing mil-default mapping while exposing the MOA variant per Appendix G #12. Option A per the Phase 5 directive (vs. schema change for list-valued reticle_ids).

Phase 5 Class C dual-reticle split: Mark 5HD 7-35x56 ships in BOTH Tremor3 (existing scope row mapped to loadout_mil_tree_flare) AND TMOA-HD (this row, mapped to loadout_moa_tree_flare). Option A per the Phase 5 directive.

Phase 5 Class C dual-reticle split: VX-Freedom 3-9x40 ships in BOTH Duplex (existing scope row mapped to pd_plex) AND Boone & Crockett BDC (this row, mapped to loadout_hunting_bdc). Option A per the Phase 5 directive.
